narrative:
DRIVER
USD leads D1 CSM at 100 with CHF divergence flagged as the critical short-term signal: CHF H4 CSM collapses to 20 versus D1 at 79, a 59-point gap, confirming CHF fading against USD strength driven by Fed hawkish repricing and US-Iran geopolitical flows into dollar. USDCHF is the primary expression with H4 Strong Sell flipping to buy pressure as CHF unwinds, confirmed by EURUSD negative correlation at -0.89 and GBPUSD H4 Buy at +4 amplifying dollar strength across the EUR and GBP cluster. CAD H4 CSM accelerates 74 versus D1 at 33, a 41-point divergence, meaning USDCAD upside faces near-term H4 resistance despite D1 buy signal.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: AUDJPY CMP 58 with H4 72 rising sharply, AUDUSD CMP 51 pushing higher on H4 72
Fading: EURUSD CMP 42 with D1 momentum 23 accelerating lower, NZDUSD CMP 45 with D1 29 falling

WATCH
Strait of Hormuz headline escalation hits WTI above 96, CADJPY directional reversal to buy as CAD energy bid overrides current H4 sell signal
UK gilt yield move intraday affects GBPJPY given +0.82 GBPUSD correlation amplifying any GBP leg lower
EUR H4 CSM rising to 64 versus D1 at 6 triggers EURUSD short squeeze if price reclaims H4 structure above current neutral zone

themes.theme: US-Iran geopolitical escalation and Strait of Hormuz risks
Oil price recovery on Middle East tensions
Fed hawkish rate outlook and USD strength
Risk-on sentiment persists despite geopolitical concerns
ECB hawkishness fails to support EUR versus USD
UK gilt yields decline, pressuring GBP
RBI and RBNZ policy stances supporting commodity currencies
Safe-haven flows into USD and JPY

key_observation: USD strength driven by twin factors: safe-haven flows from escalating Iran-US tensions and hawkish Fed rate expectations. Geopolitical risks supporting oil and JPY but offsetting risk-on sentiment in equities. EUR struggling despite ECB hawkishness as USD outperforms. Commodity currencies mixed with oil volatility.
watch: Iran-US diplomatic developments, oil price movements above 92 USD/barrel, USD/JPY above 159.00, EUR/USD below 1.0800, GBP weakness on gilt yields, RBNZ decision outcome
